Graduate Assistant (Animal Science) Job at University of Maiduguri




        
    

        
        The University of Maiduguri is dedicated to fostering intellectual growth and academic excellence through a world-class education rooted in critical thinking, innovation, and ethical leadership. Our vibrant learning environment encourages students to realize their full potential, guided by renowned faculty and enriched by diverse perspectives that prepare them to lead and excel in their chosen fields.

Beyond the classroom, we emphasize research, community engagement, and global collaboration as vital pillars of our mission. With a strong commitment to social responsibility and lifelong learning, we equip our students not only with the skills for success but also with the vision and passion to make a lasting impact in an ever-evolving world.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We invite applications from suitably qualified candidates for the position below:

Job Position: Graduate Assistant (Animal Science)

Job Location: Maiduguri, Borno
Faculty: Agriculture

Requirements


	First degree in the relevant field from University of Maiduguri or another recognised institution
	Minimum of Second Class Upper Division
	NYSC discharge or exemption certificate. 


Documents Required
Please prepare the following documents before starting your application:


	Personal Documents:
	
		Current passport photograph
		Birth certificate or declaration of age
		Indigene certificate
		National ID or International Passport.
	
	
	Academic Documents:
	
		Primary School Certificate
		O'Level Certificate (WAEC/NECO)
		Tertiary Institution Certificates
		NYSC Discharge Certificate.
	
	


Note


	All applicants must possess the required qualifications before the closing date of the application.
	Candidates will be required to present original copies of all credentials during the interview process.

  "description": "The SRS Collection stands as a beacon of premier luxury, health, and wellness hospitality, celebrated for crafting bespoke wellness and lifestyle retreats tailored to discerning business and leisure travelers alike. Withillustrious properties nestled in central Lagos.

Job Position: Facility Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly experienced and proactive Facility Manager to oversee the maintenance, safety, and overall functionality of our property.
	The ideal candidate will ensure all building systems, equipment, and infrastructure operate efficiently while maintaining high standards that support exceptional guest experience and seamless operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day maintenance and operations of the facilities, including public areas, and back-of-house
	Ensure all mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC systems are functioning optimally
	Develop and implement preventive maintenance programs to minimize downtime and service disruptions
	Supervise and manage facility staff, including technicians and external contractors
	Conduct routine inspections of the premises to ensure safety, cleanliness, and operational efficiency
	Coordinate timely repairs and maintenance to avoid disruption to guests and operations
	Manage relationships with vendors and service providers, ensuring quality service and cost-effectiveness
	Monitor and control facility-related budgets and expenses
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, fire, and environmental regulations
	Oversee utilities management (power, water, waste) to ensure efficiency and cost control
	Support renovation projects, upgrades, and space optimization initiatives
	Maintain accurate records of maintenance activities, inspections, and compliance reports.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Engineering, Facility Management, or a related field
	4 - 7 years of experience in facility management, preferably within a hospitality environment
	Strong knowledge of hotel facility systems (HVAC, electrical, plumbing, fire safety systems, etc.)
	Proven experience managing maintenance teams and external vendors
	Good understanding of hospitality standards and guest service expectations
	Knowledge of health, safety, and regulatory requirements
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and facility management systems.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;srsrecruitment2025@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as subject of the mail.",

Facility Manager Job at SRS Collection




        
    

        
        The SRS Collection stands as a beacon of premier luxury, health, and wellness hospitality, celebrated for crafting bespoke wellness and lifestyle retreats tailored to discerning business and leisure travelers alike. Withillustrious properties nestled in central Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Facility Manager

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly experienced and proactive Facility Manager to oversee the maintenance, safety, and overall functionality of our property.
	The ideal candidate will ensure all building systems, equipment, and infrastructure operate efficiently while maintaining high standards that support exceptional guest experience and seamless operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day maintenance and operations of the facilities, including public areas, and back-of-house
	Ensure all mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC systems are functioning optimally
	Develop and implement preventive maintenance programs to minimize downtime and service disruptions
	Supervise and manage facility staff, including technicians and external contractors
	Conduct routine inspections of the premises to ensure safety, cleanliness, and operational efficiency
	Coordinate timely repairs and maintenance to avoid disruption to guests and operations
	Manage relationships with vendors and service providers, ensuring quality service and cost-effectiveness
	Monitor and control facility-related budgets and expenses
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, fire, and environmental regulations
	Oversee utilities management (power, water, waste) to ensure efficiency and cost control
	Support renovation projects, upgrades, and space optimization initiatives
	Maintain accurate records of maintenance activities, inspections, and compliance reports.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering, Facility Management, or a related field
	4 - 7 years of experience in facility management, preferably within a hospitality environment
	Strong knowledge of hotel facility systems (HVAC, electrical, plumbing, fire safety systems, etc.)
	Proven experience managing maintenance teams and external vendors
	Good understanding of hospitality standards and guest service expectations
	Knowledge of health, safety, and regulatory requirements
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and facility management systems.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the mail.

	As Head, Projects &amp; Works, you will provide strategic leadership for the planning, execution, and delivery of infrastructure and technical projects across the organization.
	You will ensure projects are delivered safely, on schedule, within budget, and to required quality standards.


Your Contribution will include


	Project Planning &amp; Delivery Oversight: Lead the planning, monitoring, and execution of network expansion, upgrade, rehabilitation, and third-party infrastructure projects.
	Infrastructure Development &amp; Technical Design: Provide civil and engineering input during project conceptualization and planning, review and approve technical drawings, and ensure infrastructure designs align with network expansion and operational needs.
	Project Monitoring &amp; Quality Assurance: Track physical and financial project progress, conduct site inspections, verify contractor deliverables, and ensure strict compliance with approved specifications, safety standards, and technical requirements.
	Risk Management &amp; Compliance: Develop risk management plans for infrastructure investments, identify potential technical, financial, and delivery risks, and implement mitigation strategies to safeguard project success.
	Data Management &amp; Reporting: Supervise the maintenance of project databases and as-built drawings while producing weekly, monthly, quarterly, and annual reports to support leadership decision-making.


Minimum&nbsp;Requirements


	This opportunity is a Managerial role responsible for overseeing the planning and delivery of infrastructure and technical projects across the organization.
	This position reports directly to the Chief Technical Officer.


Successful applicants will typically possess the following criteria:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or its equivalent in Civil Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related discipline.
	A Master&rsquo;s degree in Engineering, Project Management, or Business Administration (MBA) is an added advantage.
	Professional engineering certification and membership in recognized bodies such as COREN, NSE etc. is required.
	Relevant project management certifications such as PMP or PRINCE2 is an added advantage.
	A minimum of 10 years of progressive engineering and project management experience, with significant exposure to large-scale infrastructure, utility, or power sector.


This position offers the incredible opportunity to:


	Lead large-scale infrastructure and power distribution projects that directly improve network reliability and service delivery.
	Shape the expansion and modernization of electricity distribution infrastructure across the organization.
	Work closely with senior leadership to influence capital investment planning and technical strategy.
	Develop expertise in utility infrastructure project management, risk mitigation, and regulatory compliance.
	Build and lead high-performing technical teams while driving operational excellence and continuous improvement in project delivery.


Skills and Competencies:


	The role affords you to develop technical and behavioral competencies, that is, skills, knowledge, and behaviors required to successfully perform in the role and contribute to the organization&#39;s goals.
	Electricity Industry Regulatory Knowledge
	Project Management
	Civil Engineering Design
	Technical Works / Infrastructure Construction Management
	Technical Project Quality Assurance and Control
	Safety and Health Compliance
	Customer Centricity
	Risk Management
	Change Management
	Entrepreneurship
	Organizational Learning
	People Leadership.

Head of Legal Job at Wagon Wheel Limited




        
    

        
        Wagon Wheel Limited, established in 2014, is a leading luxury real estate development company in Africa, specializing in the creation and sale of high-end properties with bespoke interiors, premium finishes, and flexible payment plans. Rooted in the values of accountability, quality, ownership, innovation, and integrity, the company aims to deliver exceptional, fully serviced, move-in-ready homes. With a focus on customer-centric experiences and a strategy combining innovative and traditional business models, Wagon Wheel Ltd ensures peace of mind for its clients. The company is committed to enhancing stakeholder value while providing the finest luxury real estate experience.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Legal

Job Location: Utako, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a highly experienced and commercially driven Head of Legal to lead all legal, regulatory, and compliance matters across the company’s operations.
	This role will serve as the principal legal advisor to the CEO and management, ensuring that all transactions, partnerships, and projects are structured, executed, and protected at the highest professional standard.


Key Responsibilities
Legal Advisory & Strategy:


	Act as the chief legal advisor to the company.
	Provide clear, practical, and business-focused legal guidance on all company matters.
	Structure and advise on:
	Joint Venture Agreements (JVAs)
	Development and construction contracts
	Investment and financing arrangements


Contract Management:


	Draft, review, and negotiate all contracts including:
	Construction and turnkey agreements
	Consultant and vendor contracts
	Property sales and off-plan agreements
	Ensure all contracts are robust, enforceable, and risk-protected


Real Estate & Land Matters:


	Lead all legal aspects of:
	Land acquisition and title verification
	Due diligence and title perfection
	Regulatory approvals and documentation
	Liaise with government authorities and land stakeholders


Dispute Resolution:


	Manage all disputes, claims, and litigation matters
	Lead negotiations and settlements where required
	Oversee external counsel to ensure senior-level handling at all times


Compliance & Governance:


	Establish and maintain strong compliance systems
	Ensure adherence to all applicable laws and regulations
	Support corporate governance and board processes


Strategic & International Support:


	Support cross-border transactions and expansion initiatives
	Work with international advisors where necessary
	Provide legal structure for new markets and partnerships


Requirements


	LL.B (Bachelor of Laws)
	BL (Nigerian Law School)
	Minimum 10–15 years post-call experience
	Strong expertise in:
	
		Real Estate Law
		Corporate & Commercial Law
		Contract Negotiation
		Dispute Resolution
	
	


Preferred Profile:


	Background in a top-tier law firm or as Head of Legal
	Proven experience handling high-value transactions and complex deals
	Strong exposure to:
	
		Real estate development projects
		Financial institutions and structured deals
		Government and regulatory bodies
	
	


Core Competencies:


	Strong commercial awareness
	Excellent negotiation skills
	High attention to detail
	Ability to operate independently at executive level
	Integrity, discretion, and sound judgment


Key Expectations:


	Provide fast, decisive, and practical legal guidance
	Anticipate and mitigate risks proactively
	Maintain full ownership of all legal matters
	Operate as a trusted advisor to leadership


Compensation


	Competitive salary (based on experience)
	Performance-based incentives
	Clear pathway to Legal Director / Group Head of Legal


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: This role is critical to ensuring that Wagon Wheel operates with legal strength, transactional clarity, and risk control, supporting its growth across Nigeria and international markets.
